title: Pronouns and Structures in Surahs: I‘rāb Focus
summary: The session reviewed Arabic grammar through exploring pronouns (dama'ir) and their usage in Qur’anic verses, emphasizing gender agreement and contextual references. Key examples included ha, ya, and dharf constructions, with practical applications from the Quran.
key_points: Pronouns (الضمائر) refer back to the nearest noun (masculine or feminine). Example: fi-hi/fi-ha for 'in it'.
Feminine nouns like nafs (النفس) use laha (لها), despite lacking a ta' marbuta.
Dharf (circumstantial words) indicate place/time. Example: ‘alayka (عليك) from ala + ka, meaning 'upon you'.
Maf’ool bihi (object of verb) takes fatha: uhibbu Allaha (أُحِبُ ٱللَّه).
Yā’ endings hide expected fatha: kitabi (كتابي) instead of kitabaya.
questions: [00:16:32] Q: Why is 'naafiratun' used with 'fee-ha'? — A: Refers to 'my room', feminine, thus 'ha'.
